_comment: Connector-specific edits a cli-printing-press reprint must NOT clobber. check_handfixes.py asserts every marker below is still present and fails CI if a reprint dropped one.

handfixes.summary: Separate userevents client + KNOWBE4_USER_EVENT_API_KEY for the User Event API (events group); a hand-authored second-key client
handfixes.why: KnowBe4 exposes two APIs behind one CLI: the Reporting API (KNOWBE4_API_KEY) and a separate User Event API on its own host with its own key (KNOWBE4_USER_EVENT_API_KEY). The `events` group (create/delete) talks to the User Event API via a dedicated hand-authored client package; the Reporting key cannot reach it. A reprint that drops the userevents package or its EnvKey breaks the events write surface.

handfixes.summary: `import` is a write but uses the MAIN Reporting key (flags.newClient), NOT the secondary User Event key; docs attribute keys per-write
handfixes.why: The natural-but-wrong mental model is 'the events sub-product is the write surface, so all writes use the events key.' FALSE: `import` is a bulk POST write that uses the MAIN Reporting key (import.go calls flags.newClient(), the primary client) - only `events create`/`events delete` use the secondary key. Two adversarial rounds were needed to get the docs right (README + SKILL.md both initially mis-attributed). The governance Write row must keep `import` attributed to the main key (no secondary-key note) while `events create` notes it needs KNOWBE4_USER_EVENT_API_KEY.

asserts.contains: `events create` (push a custom risk event; needs `KNOWBE4_USER_EVENT_API_KEY`), `import`
